verse_specific_anchors: the pair structure: cold/heat, pleasure/pain — preserved as polarity pairs of dashboard-up/dashboard-down, alert-fire/alert-clear
the temporal structure: āgama/apāya (arrival/departure) — preserved as 'signals arrive, signals depart'
titikṣā as active discipline — preserved as 'refusal to couple stability to polarity', 'deliberate practice has trained the reflexive coupling away'
anityāḥ (impermanent) — preserved as the temporal shape of sensor readings
verdict_reason: Tested against three random verses from Chapters 7, 13, and 17: BG 7.16 (catur-vidhā bhajante māṁ — four kinds worship me), BG 13.7 (amānitvam adambhitvam — humility, non-pretentiousness), BG 17.14 (deva-dvija-guru-prājña — worship of gods, brahmins, teachers). The 2.14 engineering analog (decoupling stability from sensor-input polarity) cannot equally apply to any of these. The polarity-pair structure plus the arrival-departure temporal shape is uniquely a 2.14 reading — neither the worship-categorization verses nor the qualities-of-knowledge verse have this sensor-impermanence structure. PASS.
